global.title: masterchef accumulator pattern enables per-token revenue tracking
global.content:
# masterchef accumulator pattern enables per-token revenue tracking

The POWA contracts use a well-established DeFi pattern (originating from SushiSwap's MasterChef) where a global `accRevenuePerShare` accumulator tracks total revenue accrued per unit of token supply. Each account stores a `rewardDebt` checkpoint — the accumulator value at last interaction — and `pending` unclaimed revenue.

When revenue is deposited:
```
accRevenuePerShare += (revenueAmount * PRECISION) / totalSupply
```

When an account is updated:
```
accumulated = balance * accRevenuePerShare / PRECISION
earned = accumulated - rewardDebt
pending += earned
rewardDebt = accumulated
```

This is an **on-chain implementation detail** that the off-chain document model should NOT replicate. The document model instead captures the business-level view: epoch config, total revenue, per-epoch allocation percentages, and claim events. The accumulator math runs on-chain; the document model stores inputs and outputs.

Key insight for modeling: the document model needs "Revenue Configuration" (inputs) and "Distribution Records" (outputs) but NOT the accumulator state itself.

---

- [[powa document model bridges on-chain contracts with off-chain governance]]

global.description: Revenue-per-share accumulator pattern from DeFi (MasterChef) used by POWA contracts to distribute revenue proportionally to token holders
